## We're in a Bubble / Overvalued

### Andrew Feldman

> Andrew argues that the AI infrastructure buildout is not a speculative bubble but the early stages of a fundamental technological shift. The demand for compute driven by AI models and agents will continue to grow exponentially, and the current investment levels are rational given the opportunity ahead.

### Ben Horowitz

> Ben pushes back hard on AI bubble narratives. He distinguishes the dot-com bubble (1996-2000), where unit economics didn't work and businesses had no revenue, from the current AI boom, where companies are genuinely growing fast with real revenue. ### Chad Peets

> In his most recent episode, Peets discusses how AI companies like Anthropic are causing a compensation crisis in sales (the "$100M CRO bubble").
